description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that builds Debian and Fedora packages using the debian:stretch and fedora:27 Docker images. Define four jobs: build debian nightly runs apt-get update -qq, apt-get install -y devscripts pbuilder aptitude, /usr/lib/pbuilder/pbuilder-satisfydepends, dpkg-buildpackage -b -us -uc, mkdir -p artifacts, mv ../*.deb ../*.changes ../*.buildinfo artifacts/, stores the artifacts for 1 day, and runs on all branches except tags; build debian release runs the same commands, stores the artifacts for 1 year, and runs only on tag pushes; build fedora nightly runs dnf install -y make sbcl curl gzip pandoc git wget man rubygems python ruby-devel gcc redhat-rpm-config rpm-build, gem install fpm, ./configure --prefix=/usr, make SHASUM=sha256sum, temp=$(mktemp -d), make install DESTDIR="$temp", fpm -s dir -t rpm -n isoostrap -v $(date +%s) -C "$temp" -d qemu -d iproute2 -d tar, mkdir -p artifacts, mv *.rpm artifacts/, stores the artifacts for 1 day, and runs on all branches except tags; build fedora release runs the same steps but uses "$CI_COMMIT_TAG" as the package version and runs only on tag pushes.
